questions.text: What are the opening disjointed letters of Surah Ar-Rum?
questions.answer: Alif, Lam, Meem.

questions.text: What historical prediction is made in the opening of this Surah?
questions.answer: The victory of the Romans over their enemies within a few years (3 to 9 years) after their initial defeat.

questions.text: Where did the initial defeat of the Romans take place?
questions.answer: In the nearest land (Adna al-Ard).

questions.text: To whom does the decision (order) belong before and after the Roman victory?
questions.answer: To Allah alone.

questions.text: Why would the believers rejoice on the day of the Roman victory?
questions.answer: Because of the help of Allah; He helps whom He wills and He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

questions.text: Does Allah ever fail in His promise?
questions.answer: No, it is the promise of Allah, and He does not fail in His promise, but most people do not know.

questions.text: How do many people generally view the life of this world?
questions.answer: They know only the outward appearance of the worldly life, but they are heedless of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What should people reflect upon regarding their own creation and the universe?
questions.answer: That Allah did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them except in truth and for a specified term.

questions.text: What is the lesson from the travel throughout the earth regarding previous nations?
questions.answer: To see the end of those who were more powerful, who tilled the earth and built it more than they have, but were destroyed when they denied their messengers.

questions.text: What was the result for those who did evil and denied the signs of Allah?
questions.answer: The worst of outcomes, because they used to mock the signs.

questions.text: Who begins creation and then repeats it and to whom will all return?
questions.answer: Allah.

questions.text: What will be the state of the criminals on the Day the Hour is established?
questions.answer: They will be in despair (stubborn silence and grief).

questions.text: Will the 'partners' they associated with Allah help them on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: No, they will have no intercessors from among their 'partners', and they will deny those partners.

questions.text: How will the believers and the righteous be rewarded on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will be in a garden (Paradise), delighted and honored.

questions.text: At what times of the day is Allah specially exalted as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: In the evening (Masa') and in the morning (Subh).

questions.text: What are the other two times of day mentioned for praising Allah in this section?
questions.answer: In the afternoon (Ashiyyan) and when you are at noon (Zuhur).

questions.text: How does Allah bring out the living from the dead and the dead from the living?
questions.answer: As a sign of His power, just as He gives life to the earth after its death.

questions.text: What is the sign mentioned regarding the origin of human creation?
questions.answer: That He created you from dust, then suddenly you were human beings dispersing [throughout the earth].

questions.text: What is the 'sign' mentioned regarding the creation of spouses?
questions.answer: That He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquility in them; and He placed between you affection (Mawaddah) and mercy (Rahmah).

questions.text: What are the two signs of Allah mentioned regarding the heavens, the earth, and human diversity?
questions.answer: The creation of the heavens and the earth, and the diversity of your languages and your colors.

questions.text: What is the sign mentioned regarding sleep and seeking bounty?
questions.answer: Your sleep by night and day and your seeking of His bounty.

questions.text: How is the lightning described as a sign in this Surah?
questions.answer: He shows you the lightning for fear and hope, and He sends down rain from the sky.

questions.text: What happens when Allah calls people from the earth with a single call?
questions.answer: Immediately they will come forth (for resurrection).

questions.text: To whom does the 'highest attribute' (Al-Mathal al-A'la) belong in the heavens and the earth?
questions.answer: To Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

questions.text: What is the parable of the slaves mentioned to refute Shirk?
questions.answer: Would a person share his own wealth and status equally with his slaves? If not, then why associate partners with the Creator of all?

questions.text: What do the wrongdoers follow instead of knowledge?
questions.answer: They follow their own desires without knowledge.

questions.text: What is the 'Fitra' of Allah mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The natural inclination/disposition upon which Allah has created all mankind; there is no change in the creation of Allah.

questions.text: How should believers turn to Allah when facing Him in religion?
questions.answer: As Hanifs (pure monotheists), turning in repentance to Him, fearing Him, and establishing prayer.

questions.text: What does Allah say about those who 'split up their religion and became sects'?
questions.answer: Each faction rejoices in what it has; believers should avoid being among them.

questions.text: How do people react when a hardship touches them vs when they taste mercy?
questions.answer: They call upon their Lord in repentance during hardship, but when they taste mercy, some of them associate partners with Him again.

questions.text: How do people react when Allah gives them a blessing vs when an evil touches them because of their own deeds?
questions.answer: They rejoice in the blessing, but they despair when evil touches them.

questions.text: Who should be given their due right according to this Surah?
questions.answer: The relative, the needy, and the traveler; this is best for those who desire the 'Face' of Allah.

questions.text: What is the result of giving wealth in Riba (interest) to increase within the wealth of people?
questions.answer: It will not increase with Allah.

questions.text: What is the reward for giving Zakat out of a desire for Allah's 'Face'?
questions.answer: Those are the ones who will have their reward multiplied.

questions.text: What are the four actions of Allah mentioned to show His power over life and death?
questions.answer: He created you, then provided for you, then He will cause you to die, then He will give you life.

questions.text: What causes corruption (Fasad) to appear throughout the land and sea?
questions.answer: What the hands of people have earned (their sins and disobedience).

questions.text: What is the purpose of Allah allowing some of the consequences of people's actions to reach them?
questions.answer: So that they may taste part of what they have done and perhaps they will return to the right path.

questions.text: How should one set their face toward the 'correct religion'?
questions.answer: Directly and firmly, before there comes a Day from Allah that cannot be averted.

questions.text: What happens to the disbeliever and the righteous doer on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The disbeliever's disbelief is against himself, while those who did righteousness have prepared for themselves a good place.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the winds as a sign of His mercy?
questions.answer: As heralds of glad tidings, to give you a taste of His mercy, and to make the ships sail by His command.

questions.text: What was the mission of the messengers sent before Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.answer: To bring clear proofs to their people; Allah took retribution from the criminals and it was an obligation upon Him to help the believers.

questions.text: Describe the process of cloud formation and rain as mentioned in this Surah.
questions.answer: Allah sends the winds that stir up clouds, spreads them in the sky as He wills, and makes them fragments so you see the rain coming from them.

questions.text: How do people react when rain falls on them after they had been in despair?
questions.answer: They rejoice, even though they were certain of despair just before it fell.

questions.text: What is the lesson from looking at the 'effects of the mercy of Allah' on the earth?
questions.answer: How He gives life to the earth after its death; indeed, that same Allah will give life to the dead.

questions.text: How do the disbelievers react if a wind turns their crops yellow (damaging them)?
questions.answer: They remain thereafter in persistent disbelief/ingratitude.

questions.text: Can the Prophet make the dead hear or the deaf hear the call?
questions.answer: No, especially when they turn their backs and flee.

questions.text: Can the Prophet guide the blind out of their error?
questions.answer: No, he can only make those hear who believe in Allah's verses and are Muslims.

questions.text: What is the sequence of human strength and weakness described in this Surah?
questions.answer: Created in weakness, then given strength, then after strength, weakness and white hair (old age).

questions.text: What will the criminals swear on the Day of Judgment regarding the length of their stay in the world?
questions.answer: They will swear they remained but an hour.

questions.text: What will those given knowledge and faith say to the criminals on that Day?
questions.answer: They will say, 'You have remained according to the decree of Allah until the Day of Resurrection, and this is the Day of Resurrection, but you did not know.'

questions.text: Will the excuses of the wrongdoers benefit them on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: No, and they will not be asked to appease Allah.

questions.text: How does Allah seal the hearts of those who do not know?
questions.answer: By presenting every kind of example in the Quran, which they still reject as 'falsehood'.

questions.text: What is the final instruction to the Prophet in the last Ayah of Surah Ar-Rum?
questions.answer: To be patient, for the promise of Allah is truth, and not to let those who have no certainty discourage him.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'Ar-Rum'?
questions.answer: The Romans.

questions.text: What is the 'Adna al-Ard'?
questions.answer: The 'nearest land' or the 'lowest land', referring to the location of the Roman-Persian battle.

questions.text: What is the 'Face' (Wajh) of Allah?
questions.answer: A term used to describe seeking His pleasure, approval, and reward alone.

questions.text: What is the 'Mawaddah' and 'Rahmah' in marriage?
questions.answer: Affection and mercy, the two essential spiritual ingredients for a successful union.

questions.text: What is the 'diversity of colors' a sign of?
questions.answer: Allah's infinite creativity and the beauty of human difference.

questions.text: What is the 'seeking of bounty' (Ibtigha' Fadlihi)?
questions.answer: Working and searching for one's livelihood provided by Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Hanif' state?
questions.answer: The state of pure monotheism, being upright and sincere in faith.

questions.text: What is the 'Specified Term' (Ajal Musamma)?
questions.answer: The fixed time for the existence of the universe and every individual life.

questions.text: Why is the Roman victory called 'Help from Allah'?
questions.answer: Because it validated the Quranic prophecy and supported the People of the Book against idolaters.

questions.text: What is the 'outward appearance' (Zahiran) of life?
questions.answer: The material and temporary aspects of the world that people focus on while neglecting the spiritual reality.

questions.text: What is the 'highest example'?
questions.answer: The ultimate perfection that belongs only to Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'natural disposition' (Fitra)?
questions.answer: The innate human drive to recognize and worship the one true Creator.

questions.text: What is the 'Bounteous reward'?
questions.answer: The multiplication of good deeds for those who give for Allah's sake.

questions.text: What is the 'Corruption' (Fasad) on land and sea?
questions.answer: Environmental, social, and spiritual decay caused by human immorality and rejection of divine laws.

questions.text: What is the 'Correct religion' (Ad-Din al-Qayyim)?
questions.answer: The upright and unchanging path of Islam.

questions.text: What is the 'Mercy' (Rahmah) shown through rain?
questions.answer: The physical provision of water and the spiritual reminder of resurrection.

questions.text: What is the 'Retribution' (Intiqam) from criminals?
questions.answer: Allah's justice in punishing those who knowingly reject the truth and harm others.

questions.text: What is the 'Obligation' (Haqqan) to help believers?
questions.answer: A promise Allah has made to Himself to ensure the ultimate success of the faithful.

questions.text: What is the 'Breeze' (Mubashshirat) role of the winds?
questions.answer: To bring relief and the news of coming rain.

questions.text: What is the 'White hair' (Shaybah) a sign of?
questions.answer: The final stage of human life leading to the return to the Creator.

questions.text: What is the 'Certainty' (Yaqin) required for patience?
questions.answer: Absolute trust in Allah's promise which keeps one firm during trials.

questions.text: What is the 'Return' (Yarji'un) hoped for through trials?
questions.answer: Turning back to Allah and correcting one's course.

questions.text: What is the 'Appeasement' (Yusta'tabun)?
questions.answer: Being given a chance to make amends, which will not happen after death.

questions.text: What is the 'Knowledge and Faith' (Al-Ilm wal-Iman)?
questions.answer: The two foundations for understanding the reality of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Seal' (Tab'a) on hearts?
questions.answer: The loss of spiritual perception due to persistent rejection of the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'Truth' (Haqq) which Allah's promise is?
questions.answer: The undeniable reality of resurrection and judgment.

questions.text: What is the final lesson of Ar-Rum?
questions.answer: That political and natural events are all signs of Allah's power and reminders of the ultimate return to Him.
